stem: Which vehicle is allowed on a Dutch motorway (autosnelweg)?

choices.text: A moped able to do 45 km/h
A car able to sustain at least 60 km/h
A bicycle in good condition
A tractor with a top speed of 40 km/h

explanation.short: Motorways are only for motor vehicles that can sustain at least 60 km/h.
explanation.examTip: Mopeds, cyclists, pedestrians and slow traffic are always forbidden on autosnelweg and autoweg.

stem: There are no extra speed signs. What is the daytime maximum speed on a Dutch motorway between 06:00 and 19:00?

choices.text: 80 km/h
100 km/h
120 km/h
130 km/h

explanation.short: The default daytime motorway limit is 100 km/h; higher limits apply only when signed and outside daytime hours.
explanation.examTip: On an expressway (autoweg) the maximum is 100 km/h day and night.

stem: When may you stop on the shoulder (vluchtstrook) of a motorway?

choices.text: To check directions on your phone
Only for a real breakdown or emergency
To let a passenger get out
Whenever the road is quiet

explanation.short: The shoulder is reserved for genuine breakdowns and emergencies.
explanation.examTip: Switch on hazard lights and place the warning triangle 30 metres behind the vehicle.

stem: You are on the acceleration lane joining a motorway. What should you do?

choices.text: Drive slowly and force traffic to brake for you
Stop at the end of the lane and wait for a gap
Build up to motorway speed, signal left and merge into a gap
Cross straight onto the left lane

explanation.short: Match the speed of motorway traffic, signal, and merge smoothly.
explanation.examTip: Traffic already on the motorway has priority, so you fit in around it.

stem: You want to leave the motorway at the next exit. When should you start braking?

choices.text: Before signalling, in the right-hand lane
On the main carriageway, well before the exit
Only once you are on the deceleration lane
After the end of the exit lane

explanation.short: Brake only after moving onto the deceleration lane, so you do not slow traffic behind.
explanation.examTip: Signal right in good time so others know you are leaving.

stem: The sign 'rijstrook eindigt' warns that your lane is ending. What is the correct way to merge?

choices.text: Race to the front and force your way in
Stop and wait for everyone else to pass
Use the zipper principle: take turns one-by-one with the next lane
Drive on the shoulder until past the obstruction

explanation.short: Where a lane ends, drivers merge one-by-one with the adjacent lane (ritsen).
explanation.examTip: Merging late but smoothly at the zipper point is fine; merging aggressively is not.

stem: You are about to enter a road tunnel in daylight. What must you do with your headlights?

choices.text: Switch them off so as not to dazzle others
Use parking lights only
Switch on dipped headlights before entering
Use full beam throughout the tunnel

explanation.short: Dipped headlights are required in tunnels, even during the day.
explanation.examTip: A red cross above your lane means the lane is closed; follow the green arrows instead.

stem: At an opening bridge the red lights are flashing and the barrier is coming down. The bridge still looks clear. You should:

choices.text: Cross quickly while it is still clear
Stop behind the line and wait until lights are off and barriers fully up
Drive around the barrier on the pavement
Sound the horn and continue

explanation.short: Flashing red lights and a descending barrier mean stop and wait until both clear.
explanation.examTip: The same applies at level crossings: never cross while red lights flash.

stem: A train has just passed an unguarded level crossing. You should:

choices.text: Cross immediately, since the train has gone
Look both ways and only cross when sure no second train is coming
Sound the horn while crossing
Reverse and find another route

explanation.short: Trains can come in either direction; always check both ways before crossing.
explanation.examTip: Trains always have priority — at a level crossing you yield, even without barriers.

stem: While performing a special manoeuvre (such as reversing or leaving a parking spot), you must:

choices.text: Be given priority by other traffic
Yield to every other road user, including pedestrians and cyclists
Yield only to motor vehicles
Follow the right-before-left rule

explanation.short: During a special manoeuvre you give way to all other road users.
explanation.examTip: Pulling away from the kerb counts as a special manoeuvre too.

choices.text: In a quiet residential street
On a motorway, expressway or in a tunnel
In a supermarket car park
On a 30 km/h road

explanation.short: Reversing is forbidden on motorways, expressways and inside tunnels.
explanation.examTip: If you miss an exit on a motorway, you must drive on to the next one — never reverse.

choices.text: On every 50 km/h road
On a motorway, expressway, or where a continuous line divides the lanes
Only at junctions
Only between 22:00 and 06:00

explanation.short: U-turns are forbidden on motorways, expressways, across continuous lines, and where signs forbid them.
explanation.examTip: Even where allowed, you must yield to all traffic before turning.

stem: Which vehicles may NOT use a Dutch motorway (autosnelweg)?

choices.text: Cars, trucks and buses
Vehicles unable to maintain at least 60 km/h, plus mopeds and bicycles
Any vehicle with a trailer
Foreign-registered cars

explanation.short: Motorways are off-limits to slow vehicles (<60 km/h capacity), mopeds, bicycles, pedestrians and farm equipment.
explanation.examTip: The blue sign with two stacked lanes marks the start of a motorway.

stem: You approach the end of a motorway on-ramp. What is the correct technique?

choices.text: Stop at the end of the slip road and wait for a long gap
Match the speed of motorway traffic and merge into a gap
Drive on the hard shoulder until it is empty
Force motorway traffic to brake for you

explanation.short: Use the full length of the slip road to reach motorway speed, then merge smoothly into a gap.
explanation.examTip: Drivers already on the motorway should make space, but the duty to give way is on the merging driver.

stem: Your car breaks down inside a long road tunnel. What should you do first?

choices.text: Open the bonnet to cool the engine
Switch on hazard lights, move passengers to safety and use an emergency phone
Leave the car and walk back along the tunnel
Continue driving slowly until you exit

explanation.short: Hazard lights on, evacuate to a safe refuge, then use the tunnel emergency phone — these reach control directly.
explanation.examTip: Do not turn the engine off if traffic must move past you; keep ventilation running where possible.

stem: You need to reverse around a corner on a quiet street. What is the safest approach?

choices.text: Rely only on the reversing camera
Look over your shoulder and use mirrors; have a helper if visibility is poor
Use the horn continuously
Reverse as fast as possible to clear the corner

explanation.short: Direct vision over the shoulder plus mirrors gives you full coverage; a banksman fills any blind spot you cannot see.
explanation.examTip: If you cannot see the area behind, get out and check before moving.
